anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Radical Jodo Shinshu, especially Tannisho chapters 8 and 10, where nembutsu is not the practitioner's practice or good act and no working is true working.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in universality and requires a refusal-category. The idea remains useful as a coding heuristic, but not as a claim that all traditions perform a handoff.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If even hearing, saying, entrusting, and gratitude are Amida's working rather than trained human functions, then the analyst's transfer language may smuggle self-power into a tradition built to reject that framing. Dogen also strains the model because practice-realization collapses bridge and arrival rather than transferring ownership from one holder to another.

claim_units: Effort and gift should be modeled as timed transfer rather than fixed opposition.
Traditions train functions such as receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, and integration, then may forbid ordinary-self ownership of those same functions.
Other-power, direct-introduction, effort-centered, and practice-realization paths differ by transfer point, support holder, credited source, forbidden claimant, safeguard, and repair.
This can become a coding rubric for manuals and practitioner interviews.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Methods that move agency from therapist or group to client, or from willpower to acceptance, should show special safeguards at transfer points, such as relapse planning, integration, alliance repair, and warnings against premature independence.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Identity-changing work trains capacities that later must be relocated from client ego, therapist, group, medication, protocol, insight, or ongoing practice.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Ordinary skill acquisition shows the same warnings, or outcomes are fully explained by severity, alliance, dosage, social support, and resources without any transfer-specific signal.

exact_claim: The useful comparative variable is not effort versus gift on a fixed scale, but the stage at which a trained function becomes unclaimable by the practitioner, who or what then carries it, and which safeguards appear when that transfer fails.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Pure Land texts should warn against calculation, Dzogchen texts against contrivance and premature recognition, Soto texts against gaining idea, early Buddhist texts against appropriation of aggregates or attainment ownership, and Gita-style karma-yoga texts against fruit ownership.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Manuals should mark transfer points with special safeguards rather than generic warnings.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind stage-coded manual analysis across at least three texts per tradition.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings cluster by institution, teacher temperament, ethics, or historical polemic rather than by transfer point.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Interviewees can distinguish I cultivate, I entrust, recognition happens, and just sitting enacts, without being prompted with the rubric.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Dual-trained practitioners should report shifts in permitted first-person grammar when changing methods.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Micro-phenomenological interviews with dual-trained practitioners and teachers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners report no felt shift, or shifts are fully explained by vocabulary, teacher style, temperament, or institutional identity.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Two practices with the same broad credit type but different transfer timing show different safeguards and repair instructions.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The handoff rubric should add predictive value beyond Pargament-style responsibility allocation and the local capacity ledger.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater coding of matched pairs such as Soto and Dzogchen, Pure Land and Christian grace, Theravada and karma-yoga.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Support locus and credit-distribution coding predict the same outcomes without needing handoff timing.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Self-power calculation is repaired by hearing or entrusting, contrivance by relaxing into introduced awareness, gaining idea by just-sitting correction, and attainment ownership by no-self or humility teachings.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Failed transfers should produce type-specific repair practices.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Teacher interviews and case reports from retreat, pastoral, and sangha settings.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Repair practices do not map to transfer failures, or all traditions prescribe the same repairs for the same student problems.

literature_search_queries: Pargament self-directing deferring collaborative religious coping styles problem solving responsibility God

Shinran Tannisho chapter 1 nembutsu mind arises Amida working
Longchen Rabjam Trekcho instruction recognition effort conduct vows
Dogen practice-realization not means to end shusho
Buddhist raft parable abandon teachings MN 22 upaya
Engler You must be somebody before you can be nobody no-self practice
religious coping styles locus of responsibility God collaborative deferring self-directing

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds stage-level transfer, contemplative manuals, forbidden claimant, safeguard, and repair fields rather than measuring coping style in stress response.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction. The main variable is not new, though the manual-coding form may extend it.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for allocating responsibility among self, God, and collaboration. This already formalizes who carries agency and how responsibility is distributed.
near_neighbors.source: Pargament et al., religious problem-solving styles, summarized at https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC1630686/ and https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate generalizes beyond Japanese Buddhism and asks where the transfer occurs for each trained function.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction for the effort-gift architecture, moderate reduction for stage-sensitive coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for treating self-power, other-power, and non-dualism as structured alternatives rather than a simple binary.
near_neighbors.source: Steve Bein, Self Power, Other Power, and Non-Dualism in Japanese Buddhism, https://philpapers.org/rec/BEISPO

near_neighbors.difference: Tannisho is a primary instance, not a comparative rubric. It may actually break the model by refusing the idea that a human function is first trained and then handed off.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High reduction and a major anomaly.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact for forbidden claimant language. The nembutsu is said to arise from Other Power, and even disciple ownership is rejected.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns this into one value in a broader cross-tradition rubric.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The stage distinction is already present inside the practice manual.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for direct introduction: the text rejects effort, contrivance, and grasping at the recognition point while prescribing investigation, guru reliance, vows, devotion, conduct, and stabilization.

near_neighbors.difference: Dogen is one type within the proposed rubric, not the whole comparative model.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the practice-realization category.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for collapsing practice and realization, removing practice as a bridge to later gain.
near_neighbors.source: Dogen practice-realization, St Andrews Encyclopaedia of Theology, https://www.saet.ac.uk/Buddhism/Dogen and Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/japanese-zen/

near_neighbors.difference: The raft model is about non-clinging to the Dhamma after crossing, not a full transfer ledger of capacities, support holders, and repair practices.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction for the handoff-after-use pattern.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for a method or teaching that must not be possessed after its work is done.
near_neighbors.source: MN 22, Alagaddūpama Sutta, raft parable, https://suttacentral.net/mn22/en/bodhi

near_neighbors.difference: Engler focuses on ego strength and developmental risk, not claimant transfer across gift, effort, and direct-recognition paths.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to high reduction for the train-capacity-then-dissolve-self pattern.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for the claim that psychological capacities may be prerequisites for practices that later undermine self-identification.
near_neighbors.source: Jack Engler, You Have to Be Somebody Before You Can Be Nobody, https://inquiringmind.com/article/0501_1_engler-interview/

near_neighbors.difference: The current idea integrates them as a timed handoff point and makes stage more explicit.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Very high internal reduction. Publish only as a consolidation or revision unless the new rubric predicts cases the prior rubrics did not.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact internal prior. These already name capacity location, forbidden claimant, support locus, and credit distribution.
near_neighbors.source: Local Lumenary priors: Capacity Ledger of Gift and Effort, Forbidden Claimant Rubric, Grain of Capacity, Credit Distribution as a Practice Variable

next_loop_instructions: Merge this with Capacity Ledger, Forbidden Claimant Rubric, and Credit Distribution unless a separability test shows independent predictive value.
Add rubric values for no-transfer, collapsed-transfer, continuous-transfer, analyst-frame-refused, and undecidable.

Code primary manuals before broad tradition labels, beginning with SN 45.8, Tannisho chapters 1, 6, 8, and 11, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Bendowa or Dogen practice-realization sources, and one Christian grace manual.
Do not publish as metaphysics. Publish only as an interpretive and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Run practitioner interviews that ask for actual student failure cases and repair instructions, not agreement with the model.
Use inter-rater coding to test whether transfer timing predicts safeguards beyond support locus and credit distribution.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.53 to about 0.44. The rubric is generative, but its parts are heavily anticipated by religious coping styles, self-power and other-power discourse, Dogen, Dzogchen manuals, the raft and upaya pattern, Engler, and local Lumenary priors. Treat as a consolidation unless inter-rater coding shows new predictive value.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say the idea is obvious stage pedagogy, already covered by self-power and other-power, grace and works, upaya, no-gaining, or religious coping. Pure Land teachers say the transfer frame itself is doctrinally misleading.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners say the rubric predicts concrete failure points and repair instructions better than effort-gift language, especially in cross-training cases and direct-introduction settings.
questions: Does the handoff-point rubric describe something you actively teach, or does it rename familiar self-power, other-power, grace, effort, and no-gaining language?
Where in your training sequence does a student become especially likely to claim what the practice says cannot be claimed?
Does naming support holder, credited source, forbidden claimant, safeguard, and repair change how you would guide a student?
relevant_practitioners: Christian theologians of grace and sanctification
Sufi teachers familiar with fana and baqa
Psychologists of religion familiar with religious coping styles

primary_pair: Shinran, Tannisho, A Record in Lament of Divergences, https://shinranworks.com/related-works-by-other-authors/a-record-in-lament-of-divergences/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model risks imposing a staged transfer on traditions that deny transfer, collapse before and after, or shift grammar by genre and audience. Longchenpa's Trekcho gives recognition without contrived effort but also gives preliminaries, devotion, vows, conduct, and post-recognition practice, which means production is denied only at some moments, not globally.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The search found close prior structures in religious coping styles, self-power and other-power, Dogen practice-realization, Dzogchen direct introduction, Engler on ego strength before no-self, the Buddhist raft and upaya pattern, and local Lumenary capacity-ledger and credit-distribution work. I did not find the exact field list as a cross-tradition coding sheet, but the core agency-allocation and means-end reversal are well established.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: SN 45.8 makes right effort operational and practitioner-facing: desire, endeavor, persistence, intent, and maintenance of skillful qualities are all assigned to the monk's practice field. Tannisho reverses the claimant grammar: nembutsu is not a practice or good act when it arises from Other Power, and claims such as my disciples are rejected because the working belongs to Amida. The comparison supports a real difference in claimant policy, but Tannisho is not a late handoff after training. It often refuses self-claim from the entrance, so the model needs a no-transfer or analyst-frame-refused value.

title: The Handoff Point Is The Variable
unlike_statement: Unlike Pargament, self-power and other-power debates, Dogen practice-realization, and local Lumenary credit models, this idea says the decisive unit is a timed transfer for each required function, with observable safeguards and repairs at that transfer point.
